assertion_results.text: A base error class (AppError or similar) exists with properties: code, message, statusCode, category, and cause
assertion_results.evidence: No base error class defined. Routes use ad-hoc res.status(404).json({ error: 'Not found' }) patterns with no shared error structure.

assertion_results.text: At least 5 error subclasses are defined covering: validation (400), authentication (401), authorization (403), not-found (404), and conflict (409)
assertion_results.evidence: No error subclasses defined. All errors are inline res.status().json() calls with inconsistent formats.

assertion_results.text: Each error class has a stable string error code (e.g., ERR_NOT_FOUND_USER) not just an HTTP status number
assertion_results.evidence: No error codes used. Errors identified only by HTTP status codes. Response bodies use free-text messages like 'User not found' and 'Invalid input'.

assertion_results.text: A centralized error middleware function handles all errors and produces a consistent JSON response format
assertion_results.evidence: No centralized error middleware. Each route handles its own errors with try/catch blocks producing different response formats.

assertion_results.text: Error responses include a requestId or correlationId field for debugging
assertion_results.evidence: No requestId or correlationId in any error response. No request ID generation or propagation.

assertion_results.text: User-facing error responses do not contain stack traces, file paths, or internal implementation details
assertion_results.evidence: The catch block in POST /api/users returns res.status(500).json({ error: err.message }) which exposes raw MongoDB error messages including collection names and index details.

assertion_results.text: Structured error logging includes at minimum: error code, message, stack trace, correlation ID, and request path
assertion_results.evidence: Error logging uses console.log('Error:', err) with no structured format, no correlation ID, and no request context.

assertion_results.text: The error response JSON schema is consistent across all endpoints with at least code and message fields
assertion_results.evidence: Inconsistent formats: GET /:id returns { error: 'Not found' }, POST returns { message: 'Validation failed', errors: [...] }, DELETE returns { msg: 'User deleted' } on success and { error: err.message } on failure.
